mnemonics.title: PIE Sign Rule
mnemonics.content: Signs alternate: Single +, Pair -, Triple +, Quad - . Pattern: (-1)^(k-1) where k = number of sets in intersection. Remember: ODD sets = ODD position (3rd term) = POSITIVE sign!

mnemonics.title: Derangement Formula
mnemonics.content: D(n) = n! × round(1/e). First values: D(1)=0, D(2)=1, D(3)=2, D(4)=9, D(5)=44, D(6)=265. Memorize: 0,1,2,9,44,265 - sounds like a phone number!

mnemonics.title: Onto Function Count
mnemonics.content: Onto functions from m to n: n^m - C(n,1)(n-1)^m + C(n,2)(n-2)^m - ... + (-1)^(n-1)C(n,n-1)(1)^m. Think: SUBTRACT missing images one by one!

acronyms.usage: Counting union of sets by alternating sum of intersections
Number of permutations with no fixed points, denoted !n
Number of ways to partition n objects into k non-empty subsets

visualTips.visualization: Start with three circles overlapping. First add all three circles' areas. Then subtract the three pairwise overlaps you added twice. Finally add back the triple overlap you subtracted too many times.
Think of an infinite series: 1 - 1 + 1/2! - 1/3! + 1/4! - ... = 1/e. The graph of D(n)/n! oscillates and converges to 0.3679 from above and below.
Picture n boxes (images) and m balls (domain). We want every box non-empty. Start with all m balls in any box (n^m), then exclude cases where at least one box is empty using PIE.

analogies.analogy: Think of n people sitting in n chairs but nobody in their own seat. It's like a 'mismatch party' where everyone must sit in the wrong seat.
Like distributing exam papers to students such that no student gets their own paper (if papers are pre-sorted by student name). Want every student to get a WRONG paper!
